title_en: The history of the human face is more than skin deep
title_zh: 人类面孔的历史远不止皮肤所见
rubric_en: An exuberant new book includes physiognomy, portraiture and plastic surgery
rubric_zh: 一本内容丰富的新书横跨相貌学、肖像艺术与整形外科

paragraphs.en: CAST YOUR mind back to this morning. The first thing you probably did after you woke up was reach for your smartphone and hold it up in front of your bleary eyes to unlock it. Out of bed, you looked in a mirror and decided whether to shave or apply makeup. Perhaps you smilingly cajoled a child to eat or joined a Zoom call (and tried not to look at yourself).
paragraphs.zh: 请回想一下今天早晨。你醒来后做的第一件事很可能是拿起手机，把它举到发胀的眼睛前解锁。下床后，你照了照镜子，决定要不要刮胡子或上妆。也许你笑着哄孩子吃饭，或者加入一场 Zoom 会议（并尽量不去看自己）。

paragraphs.en: Central to all those actions was your face: the particular shape and arrangement of your forehead, eyes, nose, mouth and chin. Those dimensions are used to identify you (on your passport, for instance, or via your phone’s Face ID). They will determine whether people trust you, fancy you or believe you are the right person for a job. If you are ever accused of a crime, they could influence whether or not the jury is inclined to find you guilty.
paragraphs.zh: 这些行为的核心都在于你的脸：你前额、眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴和下巴的特定形状与排列。这些尺寸被用来识别你（例如在护照上，或通过手机的 Face ID）。它们会影响别人是否信任你、是否对你心动，或者是否认为你是某份工作的合适人选。如果你曾被指控犯罪，它们还可能影响陪审团是否倾向于判你有罪。

paragraphs.en: In a fascinating new book, Fay Bound-Alberti, a cultural historian, gives full expression to the many meanings of the face. She peers across millennia and regions, looking at anthropology, criminology, medical imaging, portraiture and more to argue that faces may be made up of “skin, bone and muscle”, but perceptions of them are shaped by “belief systems, languages and environments”. Her interest in the subject stems in part from her own experience of prosopagnosia—better known as “face blindness”—a neurological condition that means she struggles to recognise even close family members.
paragraphs.zh: 在一本引人入胜的新书中，文化历史学家 Fay Bound-Alberti 充分展示了面孔的诸多含义。她跨越数千年和不同地区，考察了人类学、犯罪学、医学影像、肖像画等多个领域，提出面孔虽然由“皮肤、骨骼和肌肉”构成，但人们对它们的认知却受“信仰体系、语言和环境”的塑造。她对这个主题的兴趣部分源自她本人患有“相貌失认症”——更常见的说法是“脸盲”——一种神经系统疾病，让她很难识别甚至亲近的家人。

paragraphs.en: Humans have long taken each other at face value. Pythagoras, a Greek philosopher, supposedly chose his pupils based on whether they looked intelligent. In the wake of Euclid’s writings on proportion and beauty—ie, the “golden ratio”—many came to believe that “ill-proportioned men” were “scoundrels”.
paragraphs.zh: 人类长期以来都对对方“以貌取人”。希腊哲学家毕达哥拉斯据说会根据学生看起来是否聪明来挑选学生。在欧几里得关于比例与美，也就是“黄金分割”的著作之后，许多人开始相信，“比例失衡的人”是“无赖”。

paragraphs.en: The notion that “faces were key to a person’s social and moral status” gave authorities in ancient Greece, Rome and Persia the idea to use mutilation as a form of punishment. Criminals’ ears and noses were sliced off; eyes were gouged out and faces branded. This association of facial difference with nefarious behaviour persists: think of how many Bond villains have had scars, burns or misshapen features.
paragraphs.zh: “面孔是决定一个人社会地位和道德地位的关键”这一观念，使古希腊、古罗马和波斯的当局产生了用毁容来施行惩罚的做法。罪犯的耳朵和鼻子会被切掉，眼睛被挖掉，面部被烙印。将面部差异与卑劣行为联系在一起的观念仍然存在：想想看，有多少“007”反派角色都带着伤疤、烫伤痕迹或畸形面容。

paragraphs.en: People have been judged on their looks for centuries, but they have had “only the vaguest notion” of what they looked like until relatively recently. In medieval times, “your identity would be defined by your role, your family, your village and your faith—not by your individual face,” Ms Bound-Alberti observes. That began to change in the Renaissance, which saw an explosion of portraiture. Artists produced works that sought to capture something of the sitter’s character, not just their social standing. Your face became the map on which your inner world could be read. Ms Bound-Alberti calls this yoking of individuality to outward appearance “facehood”.
paragraphs.zh: 几百年来，人们一直被外表所评判，但直到相对较近的时期，他们才对“他人究竟长什么样”有过“最模糊的概念”。米歇尔·鲍恩-阿尔伯蒂（Ms Bound-Alberti）指出，中世纪时期，“你的身份由你的角色、你的家族、你的村庄和你的信仰来界定——而不是由你的个人面孔界定。”这种情况在文艺复兴时期开始改变，那个时期出现了肖像创作的爆发。艺术家开始创作不仅用于展现社会地位，而是试图捕捉被描绘者性格的作品。你的脸成为一张地图，内心世界可以在其上被“阅读”。鲍恩-阿尔伯蒂将这种将个体性与外在面貌绑定的现象称为“面容性”（facehood）。

paragraphs.en: As mirrors became widespread and cheap, the face became a subject of fascination. Like Narcissus gazing in the pool, people could not resist their own reflection. Photography deepened the obsession. Scientists including Charles Darwin used the technology to codify expressions and emotions; some sought to categorise and rank groups of people.
paragraphs.zh: 当镜子变得普及且价格变得便宜时，面孔更是成为人们迷恋的对象。正如那位凝视水面的纳西瑟斯一样，人们无法抗拒自己的倒影。摄影术使这种痴迷进一步加深。包括查尔斯·达尔文在内的科学家利用这一技术来系统整理表情和情绪；一些人则试图对不同人群进行分类并排序。

paragraphs.en: It is the historical morsels that will stay with you long after you finish “The Face”. Who could fail to be intrigued by Justinian II’s gold prosthetic nose, or the fact that Darwin was almost denied passage on HMS Beagle because of his schnoz? Other passages, such as those on cosmetic surgery, will feel more familiar: advertising reinforces the belief that “our faces have become projects to be perfected.”
paragraphs.zh: 真正会在你读完《The Face》后长期留在记忆里的，是这些历史细节。谁会对查士丁尼二世的金质假鼻子，或达尔文几乎因为他的“鼻子”而差点被拒绝乘坐“野鲸号”（HMS Beagle）航行感到不感兴趣？而其他内容，比如关于美容手术的段落，会显得更熟悉：广告不断强化着“我们的面孔已成为需要被完善的项目”这一信念。

paragraphs.en: At times the reader may wish that Ms Bound-Alberti had offered more analysis alongside anecdotes. Given the book’s focus on identity, for example, it is surprising that the author skims over deepfakes: studies have found that such videos can cause profound psychological harm to the person being impersonated online. Still, this is a wide-ranging and thoughtful history. Thanks to social media, people encounter more faces than ever before; after reading this book, you will see that there is more to a face than meets the eye. ■
paragraphs.zh: 读者有时可能希望邦德-阿尔伯蒂女士能在列举轶事的同时提供更多分析。比如在本书以身份认同为重点的部分，却令人惊讶地草草带过了深度伪造技术：研究表明，这类视频在网上冒充他人可能给被冒充者带来深刻的心理伤害。不过，毫无疑问，这仍然是一部视野广阔且深思熟虑的历史著作。借助社交媒体，人们如今接触到的面孔比以往更多；读完本书后，你会发现，一张脸远不只是肉眼所见的那么简单。■

paragraphs.en: For more on the latest books, films, TV shows, albums and controversies, sign up to Plot Twist, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter
paragraphs.zh: 如要了解更多最新图书、电影、电视节目、专辑与争议话题，请订阅《Plot Twist》，我们的每周订阅制通讯。
